task: authentication failure Hey I've created an app on TD Ameritrade with a callback URL `https://127.0.0.1:8080` (as [suggested](https://developer.tdameritrade.com/content/authentication-faq) on developer pages). I am making sure I am sending the correct API_KEY and redirect_url to the authentication flow ``` with webdriver.Chrome() as driver: c = auth.client_from_login_flow(driver, apikey, redirect_uri, token_path) ``` However, I am getting the error page `a third party application` [you document](https://tda-api.readthedocs.io/en/latest/auth.html). Logging shows the port on the localhost being sent with the GET is different however ``` 2020-06-08 16:48:47,541 DEBUG GET http://127.0.0.1:57214/session/<SESSION>/url {} 2020-06-08 16:48:47,544 DEBUG http://127.0.0.1:57214 "GET /session/<SESSION>/url HTTP/1.1" 200 189 2020-06-08 16:48:47,544 DEBUG Finished Request ```

task: Summary ticket for ``hu_HU`` provider Summary ticket for issues with the ``hu_HU`` provider (I'm working on a PR to fix these). - [x] ``providers.address.street_name`` (and descendants): incorrectly capitalises street/place/etc. - [x] ``providers.address.street_address_with_county``: does not capitalise place name - [x] ``providers.automotive.license_plate``: seems to be the UK licence plate structure, the correct Hungarian licence plate regex is ``\d{3}-[A-Z]{3}`` - [x] ``provides.company.company_suffix``: misspellings and miscapitalizations (e.g. ``Zrt.`` as ``ZRT`` - [x] ``providers.job``: this could be replaced with a professions list from KSH

task: Square brackets no longer work A GET request such as `/?arr[]=1&arr[]=2` is perfectly legal and it means that you are trying to send an array of values to the endpoint. Unluckily after 0.23.3 it stopped working because requests are sent as `/?arr%5B%5D=1&arr%5B%5D=2`
context: Repository: encode/httpx; changed files: debug_detailed.py, debug_failing_test.py, debug_parse.py, debug_test.py, final_verification.py, httpx/_urlparse.py

trace_summary.text: Square brackets no longer work A GET request such as `/?arr[]=1&arr[]=2` is perfectly legal and it means that you are trying to send an array of values to the endpoint. Unluckily after 0.23.3 it stopped working because requests are sent as `/?arr%5B%5D=1&arr%5B%5D=2`
I'll help you implement the necessary changes to fix the square brackets issue in the HTTPX repository.
Observation: Your thought has been logged.
### 1.1-1.3 Problem Analysis The issue describes a regression in HTTPX where square brackets (`[]`) in query parameters are being incorrectly URL-encoded.

task: Discrepancy between README and error messages README.md states: `sudo pip3 install --upgrade stups-senza` But if you have an old version of senza installed, the reported message is: `...Please install the new one using 'sudo pip install --upgrade stups-senza'` Note that `pip3` is specified in README and `pip` is specified in the error message.
context: Repository: zalando-stups/senza; changed files: .openhands/TASKS.md, issue.md, senza/subcommands/root.py

trace_summary.text: Discrepancy between README and error messages README.md states: `sudo pip3 install --upgrade stups-senza` But if you have an old version of senza installed, the reported message is: `...Please install the new one using 'sudo pip install --upgrade stups-senza'` Note that `pip3` is specified in README and `pip` is specified in the error message.
I'll help you implement the necessary changes to fix the discrepancy between README and error messages.
Observation: Your thought has been logged.

### 1.1-1.3 Problem Analysis The issue describes a **consistency problem** between documentation and runtime error messages: - **README.md** specifies: `sudo pip3 install --upgrade stups-senza` - **Error message** states: `...Please install the new one using 'sudo pip install --upgrade stups-senza'` The **discrepancy** is that README uses `pip3` while the error message uses `pip`.
